user.title: Recorder Throttle
user.description: Throttles per-entity DB writes (via rec-* labels). Configure detection of new unthrottled heavy writers and optional auto-throttling here.

scan.name: Detection & reporting
scan.description: Periodically looks for unthrottled entities writing faster than the threshold.

data.scan_enabled: Create a Repairs notice for new heavy writers

data_description.scan_enabled: Ignored while auto-throttle is on, except for entities outside the auto-throttle scope.
data_description.scan_threshold_per_min: Write rate above which an unthrottled entity counts as a heavy writer.
data_description.scan_window_hours: Time window used to measure the write rate.

auto.name: Auto-throttle
auto.description: Let the scan apply a throttle itself instead of only reporting.

data_description.auto_throttle: When on, new heavy writers are throttled automatically; entities marked accepted are never touched.
data_description.auto_throttle_policy: Interval applied to auto-throttled entities (1, 5 or 10 minutes).
data_description.auto_throttle_scope: sensor = only sensor.* entities; all = any entity type.

info.name: Info & help
info.description: Configured entirely via the UI — no YAML needed. Throttles per-entity DB writes using rec-* labels; this dialog controls detection/reporting and the optional auto-throttle. For details, examples and FAQ, use the documentation link in this dialog header.

abort.single_instance_allowed: Already configured — only a single instance is supported.

scan.name: Detection & reporting
scan.description: Periodically looks for unthrottled entities writing faster than the threshold.

data.scan_enabled: Create a Repairs notice for new heavy writers

data_description.scan_enabled: Ignored while auto-throttle is on, except for entities outside the auto-throttle scope.
data_description.scan_threshold_per_min: Write rate above which an unthrottled entity counts as a heavy writer.
data_description.scan_window_hours: Time window used to measure the write rate.

auto.name: Auto-throttle
auto.description: Let the scan apply a throttle itself instead of only reporting.

data_description.auto_throttle: When on, new heavy writers are throttled automatically; entities marked accepted are never touched.
data_description.auto_throttle_policy: Interval applied to auto-throttled entities (1, 5 or 10 minutes).
data_description.auto_throttle_scope: sensor = only sensor.* entities; all = any entity type.

info.name: Info & help
info.description: Configured entirely via the UI — no YAML needed. Throttles per-entity DB writes using rec-* labels; this dialog controls detection/reporting and the optional auto-throttle. For details, examples and FAQ, use the documentation link in this dialog header.

init.title: Resolve unthrottled heavy writers
init.description: Pick an action — it applies to all reported entities.

menu_options.accept_all: Mark all as accepted (keep full rate, stop reporting)

hook_not_installed.title: Recorder Throttle: hook not installed
hook_not_installed.description: Recorder Throttle could not patch the recorder, so database writes are **not** being throttled (the recorder itself keeps running normally). This usually happens after a Home Assistant update changed the recorder internals — update the integration or check the log.
